## Task: Delete FileServiceOptionsTests.cs (tautological auto-property tests)
## Step: review

Delete the file `TestProject.Tests/FileServiceOptionsTests.cs` in its entirety. It contains 6 tests that assert trivial auto-property behavior on FileServiceOptions (default value, mutability, type, namespace). All of these are guaranteed by the C# compiler. FileServiceOptions is exercised behaviorally through FileServiceTests. Do not modify any other file. Verification: `dotnet build TestProject.sln` succeeds; `dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj` passes.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }

./.engin/work/1781897614881-develop/.engin-state.json:180:        "The test project has a critical compilation failure that prevents ALL tests from running. The main project's `Controllers/` directory contains only `TestController.cs` (class `TestController`), but two test files — `FilesControllerTests.cs` and `FilesControllerContractTests.cs` — reference `TestProject.Controllers.FilesController`, which does not exist. This produces a CS0246 error on build (`The type or namespace name 'FilesController' could not be found`), blocking execution of even the tests relevant to this step. The tests that are directly relevant to this task — `ProgramWiringTests.cs` (DI wiring and pipeline ordering) and `FileServiceOptionsTests.cs` (options class) — are well-written: each test is focused on a single behavior, edge cases are covered (exact-count checks, ordering constraints, placement relative to Build), regex-based source parsing is appropriate for verifying Program.cs structure, and test descriptions clearly state expected behavior. No quality issues were found in those test files themselves. The blocking issue is that the two FilesController test files must either be removed, excluded from the build, or the corresponding `FilesController` must exist in the main project before the test suite can compile.",
./.engin/work/1781897614881-develop/.engin-state.json:181:        "The test suite remains unbuildable due to the same critical issue identified previously: FilesControllerTests.cs and FilesControllerContractTests.cs reference a FilesController class that does not exist in the main project, causing CS0246 build errors. The tests directly relevant to the current task (ProgramWiringTests.cs and FileServiceOptionsTests.cs) are well-constructed and appropriate, but they cannot be executed because the test project fails to compile. Until the FilesController dependency is resolved (by either creating the class, removing the test files, or excluding them from the build), no tests can run. The implementation of the required Program.cs changes has not been performed, so the task itself is incomplete."
./.engin/work/1781897614881-develop/.engin-state.json:185:        "feedback": "Program.cs matches the required specification exactly: the FileServiceOptions Configure() binding and the IFileService->FileService AddSingleton() registration are placed immediately after AddControllers(), and the pipeline is in the exact required order (UseHttpsRedirection -> UseDefaultFiles -> UseStaticFiles -> MapControllers -> MapFallbackToFile -> Run). CreateBuilder, AddControllers, and Build are unchanged. The fully-qualified type names (TestProject.FileServiceOptions, TestProject.Services.IFileService/FileService) correctly match the existing namespaces in FileServiceOptions.cs and Services/. Verification: `dotnet build TestProject.csproj` succeeds with 0 warnings/0 errors; `dotnet run` serves index.html at `https://localhost:7146/` (default files) and falls back to index.html for `https://localhost:7146/some/deep/link` (MapFallbackToFile). The previously-reported critical blocker (test project CS0246 on FilesController) is now resolved: Controllers/FilesController.cs exists, the test project builds cleanly, and `dotnet test` passes 109/109 (ProgramWiringTests and FileServiceOptionsTests execute). The implementation correctly uses the right DI lifetime (AddSingleton) and the right options-binding API (Configure<TOptions>(GetSection)). No quality issues found in the in-scope Program.cs change. One out-of-scope residual observation is noted below and should NOT be addressed as part of this Program.cs task (WONTFIX).",
